audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim second-tablets, Levite vocation, heart-circumcision, and sojourner-justice discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for renewed tablets, ark, travel/death notices, Levite separation, Moses' intercession, covenant requirements, Divine impartiality, orphan/widow justice, and love of the sojourner.

verses.restored: At that time YHWH said to me, Hew for yourself two tablets of stone like first and come up to me on mountain, and make for yourself an ark of wood.
I will write on tablets words that were on first tablets you broke, and you shall put them in ark.
I made an ark of acacia wood and hewed two tablets of stone like first and went up mountain with two tablets in my hand.
He wrote on tablets according to first writing, ten words YHWH spoke to you at mountain from midst of fire on day of assembly, and YHWH gave them to me.
I turned and came down from mountain and put tablets in ark I made, and there they are, as YHWH commanded me.
Children of Israel set out from Beeroth-bene-jaakan to Moserah; there Aaron died and was buried, and Eleazar his son served as priest in his place.
From there they set out to Gudgodah, and from Gudgodah to Jotbathah, a land of streams of water.
At that time YHWH separated tribe of Levi to carry ark of covenant of YHWH, to stand before YHWH to serve him and bless in his name, until this day.
Therefore Levi has no portion or inheritance with his brothers; YHWH is his inheritance, as YHWH your Divine spoke concerning him.
I remained on mountain as first days, forty days and forty nights, and YHWH listened to me also that time; YHWH was unwilling to destroy you.
YHWH said to me, Rise, go for journey before people, and they shall enter and possess land I swore to their fathers to give them.

Now, Israel, what does YHWH your Divine ask from you except to fear/revere YHWH your Divine, to walk in all his ways, to love him, and to serve YHWH your Divine with all your heart and all your soul/life,
to keep commands of YHWH and his statutes that I command you today for your good?
Look, to YHWH your Divine belong heavens and heavens of heavens, earth and all that is in it.
Yet only in your fathers YHWH delighted to love them, and he chose their seed after them, you from all peoples, as this day.
Circumcise foreskin of your heart, and no longer stiffen your neck.
For YHWH your Divine, he is Divine of divines and Lord of lords, great, mighty, and fearsome Divine, who does not lift face/show partiality and does not take bribe.
He makes judgment for orphan and widow and loves sojourner, giving him food and clothing.
You shall love the sojourner, for sojourners you were in land of Egypt.
YHWH your Divine you shall fear/revere, him you shall serve, to him you shall cling, and by his name you shall swear.
He is your praise and he is your Divine, who did with you these great and fearsome things your eyes saw.
With seventy souls/lives your fathers went down to Egypt, and now YHWH your Divine has made you like stars of heavens for multitude.

translation_notes: The second tablets embody covenant renewal after rupture; restoration does not erase the broken first tablets or the labor of repair.
The itinerary in verses 6-7 differs from Numbers' sequence and requires source/chronology audit.
Levite vocation is service and dependence rather than superiority; lack of land inheritance creates obligations of communal support.
Circumcision of heart is an internal Jewish covenant metaphor and must not be used for Christian supersessionism or contempt for embodied Jewish circumcision.
Divine greatness is immediately defined through impartial justice for orphan and widow and material love for the sojourner.
Israel must love immigrants because Israel remembers being immigrant and vulnerable in Egypt.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 10 for antisemitic 'uncircumcised heart' rhetoric, clerical superiority, xenophobia, or coercive assimilation. Its test of covenant reverence is impartial justice, food and clothing for the vulnerable, and active love of the sojourner.
